title: Appendix W: Passive Gravitational Tomography of Planetary Interiors Using Tidal Waves – A Coordination-Based Approach within the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT)
description: This appendix introduces a novel method for passive geophysical exploration based on the analysis of resonant crustal responses to tidal gravitational forcing from the Moon and Sun. Within the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT), the spectrum of tidal deformations contains information about the subsurface distribution of coordination efficiency K_eff, which is directly related to rock type, fluid content (oil, gas, water), and ore bodies. A mathematical model linking surface gravimetric and seismic measurements to the depth distribution of K_eff is developed, leveraging the universal error-scaling law ε = κ_c α K_eff^{-β} (β = 0.67, κ_c = 0.33). The methodology is extended to structural health monitoring of engineered structures (skyscrapers, bridges, dams), earthquake prediction, and tsunami forecasting, demonstrating that the same physical principles enable a revolutionary new class of passive monitoring technologies. The economic viability is assessed, showing that a 20–30% reduction in dry hole drilling risk would recover deployment costs many times over.

custom.ru_title: Приложение W: Пассивная гравитационная томография планетных недр с использованием приливных волн — координационный подход в рамках YUCT
custom.ru_description: Вводится новый метод пассивной геофизической разведки на основе анализа резонансных откликов коры на приливное гравитационное воздействие Луны и Солнца. В рамках YUCT спектр приливных деформаций содержит информацию о распределении эффективности координации K_eff, которая связана с типом пород, содержанием флюидов (нефть, газ, вода) и рудных тел. Разработана математическая модель, связывающая поверхностные гравиметрические и сейсмические измерения с глубинным распределением K_eff и опирающаяся на универсальный закон ошибок ε = κ_c α K_eff^{-β} (β = 0.67). Метод также расширен на мониторинг инженерных сооружений, прогнозирование землетрясений и цунами. Оценена экономическая целесообразность.

structural_topology.description: Multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) of Appendix W on passive gravitational tomography using tidal waves and its extensions to structural monitoring and earthquake/tsunami forecasting.

fundamental_concepts.coordination_efficiency: K_eff characterizes the subsurface distribution of rock properties, fluid content, and structural integrity
fundamental_concepts.universal_error_law: ε = κ_c α K_eff^{-β}, β = 2/3, κ_c = 1/3
fundamental_concepts.tidal_tomography_principle: The spectrum of tidal deformations encodes resonant information about K_eff contrasts at depth
fundamental_concepts.quality_factor_relation: 1/Q ∝ ε ∝ K_eff^{-β}, connecting resonance sharpness to coordination efficiency
fundamental_concepts.applications: Hydrocarbon exploration, structural health monitoring, earthquake prediction, tsunami forecasting

derived_predictions.hydrocarbon_identification: Gas, oil, and water-filled reservoirs have distinct K_eff values (~6.1, ~5.2, ~4.5) producing different resonant Q-factors
derived_predictions.signal_amplitude: Tidal resonance from a gas reservoir at 2 km depth produces δg ~ 3×10^{-11} m/s², detectable with superconducting gravimeters
derived_predictions.earthquake_precursor: A 20% drop in fault K_eff yields a ~13% change in tidal response amplitude, providing a measurable precursor
derived_predictions.structural_health: A 1% stiffness loss in a building shifts modal frequencies by ~0.5%, detectable by continuous tidal monitoring
derived_predictions.tsunami_forecast: Real-time K_eff monitoring of subduction zones combined with ephemerides predicts windows of heightened probability for triggered rupture

custom.notes: Complete multilingual documentation of Appendix W, version 1.0. All languages contain full translations of the mathematical model, tables, and references. The universal exponent β = 2/3 (0.6667) is explicitly included in the keywords for discoverability. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'.
